summary: D20(b) periodic re-verify BLOCKED — deepseek anonymous chat no longer available. chat.deepseek.com now redirects unauthenticated visitors straight to /sign_in. No guest chat affordance in DOM. Cache-bust did NOT bypass (gate is server-side, not cache-side). Test PC has no DeepSeek credentials → prompt path never exercised. INCONCLUSIVE — not regression but cannot confirm DONE state.

ui.dom_observation: Only email, password, login, signup, Google OAuth, Apple OAuth elements present. No chat textarea reachable.

network.post_url: N/A — no POST to inference endpoint

evidence.dispatch_reality_delta: Dispatch claimed 'no auth required for deepseek anonymous chat'. Actual state contradicts this — DeepSeek now requires login. Likely changed since 2026-04-20 last verify, or earlier tests relied on stored session that has since expired.

screenshots: files/657/after.png (same image — no 'after' exists, prompt never sent)

recommendation.primary_diagnosis: DeepSeek anonymous access has been removed (server-side change, post 2026-04-20). D20(b) probe in current form is not executable from test PC.
next_steps: Option A: Provide stored DeepSeek session credentials to test PC
Option B (recommended): Switch to auth-bypassing network-only canary — preserves mission-protective property without credential dependency
Option C: Demote deepseek from D20(b) periodic rotation until anonymous access returns
User-side action required: decide which option for cycle 98
recommendation.mission_critical: No regression detected (inconclusive run). Production deepseek pipeline state unchanged.
recommendation.checklist_impact: D20(b) deepseek probe needs revision. Cycle 98 entry blocked on this item until methodology change.
